title: verbascum × cephalariense
intro: Verbascum × cephalariense, also known as the mullein, is a hybrid plant species in the figwort family, characterized by its large, yellow flowers and woolly leaves. The medicinal properties of Verbascum × cephalariense are attributed to its high mucilage content, which has been used to soothe and calm various health issues. The plant is known to have several health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and even aid in wound healing. Verbascum × cephalariense is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
